zh.lifeSummary: 當Jeanne de Bourgogne Reine de France於大約1293年出生在法國時，她的父親Duque Roberto II de Borgoña46歲，母親Princesa Inés de Francia Duquesa de Borgoña34歲。她於1313年7月13日與Philippe VI de Valois Roi de France在法國瑟-吉馬內豐泰布利奧結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1349年12月12日在法國i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲享年57歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jeanne de Bourgogne Reine de France于大约1293年出生在法国时，她的父亲Duque Roberto II de Borgoña46岁，母亲Princesa Inés de Francia Duquesa de Borgoña34岁。她于1313年7月13日与Philippe VI de Valois Roi de France在法国瑟-吉馬內豐泰布利奧结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1349年12月12日在法国i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲享年57岁。

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bourgogne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Bourgoin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphee, Alyre, Andre, Armand, Aurore, Celina, Emile, Gilberte, Jacques, Laurent, Lucienne, Olivier.

variant of Bourgouin (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a habitational name for someone from Burgundy (Old French Bourgogne), a region of eastern France having Dijon as its center. The area was invaded by the Burgundii, an ancient Germanic tribe from whom it takes its name, in about AD 480. The duchy of Burgundy, created in 877 by Charles II, King of the Western Franks, was extremely powerful in the later Middle Ages, especially under Philip the Bold (1342–1404; duke from 1363). Compare Bourgoyne and Bourguignon .

nameMeanings.name: Jeanne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jeanne de Bourgogne Reine de France was born about 1293, in France, her father, Duque Roberto II de Borgoña, was 46 and her mother, Princesa Inés de Francia Duquesa de Borgoña, was 34. She married Philippe VI de Valois Roi de France on 13 July 1313, in Fontainebleau, Seine-et-Marne, France. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 12 December 1349, in Paris, Île-de-France, France, at the age of 57.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jeanne de Bourgogne Reine de France est née environ 1293, à France, son père, Duque Roberto II de Borgoña, avait 46 ans et sa mère, Princesa Inés de Francia Duquesa de Borgoña, avait 34 ans. Elle a épousé Philippe VI de Valois Roi de France le 13 juillet 1313, à Fontainebleau, Seine-et-Marne, France. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 12 décembre 1349, à Paris, Île-de-France, France, à l’âge de 57 ans.

Sie hat am 13. Juli 1313 in Fontainebleau, Seine-et-Marne, Frankreich Philippe VI de Valois Roi de France geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 12. Dezember 1349 im Alter von 57 Jahren in Paris, Île-de-France, Frankreich.

ko.lifeSummary: Jeanne de Bourgogne Reine de France(이)가 대략 1293년 프랑스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Duque Roberto II de Borgoña(은)는 46세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Princesa Inés de Francia Duquesa de Borgoña(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 1313년 7월 13일 프랑스 세이인-트-마니 폰테인블리에서 Philippe VI de Valois Roi de France(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1349년 12월 12일 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 파리스에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jeanne de Bourgogne Reine de France nasceu aproximadamente 1293, em França, seu pai, Duque Roberto II de Borgoña, tinha 46 anos e sua mãe, Princesa Inés de Francia Duquesa de Borgoña, tinha 34 anos. Ela casou-se com Philippe VI de Valois Roi de France em 13 de julho de 1313, em Fontainebleau, Seine-et-Marne, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jeanne de Bourgogne Reine de France родилась около 1293 г. в местности Франция, её отцу по имени Duque Roberto II de Borgoña было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Princesa Inés de Francia Duquesa de Borgoña, 34. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Philippe VI de Valois Roi de France 13 июля 1313 г. в местности фонтеинебло, сине-ет-марн, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 12 декабря 1349 г. в местности пэрис, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция в возрасте 57 лет.

Se casó con Philippe VI de Valois Roi de France el 13 de julio de 1313, en Fontainebleau, Seine-et-Marne, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas.
